title: Virgin Galactic Holdings Inc (SPCE) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Highlights: Navigating Toward Commercial Spaceflight
text: Virgin Galactic reported its first-quarter 2026 financial results on May 14, 2026, showing the company's progress toward commercial spaceflight operations despite remaining in a pre-revenue stage. The aerospace company generated $200,000 in revenue from access fees tied to future astronauts. Operating expenses totaled $66 million, representing a 26% year-over-year reduction. The net loss improved by 23% to $65 million compared to $84 million in the same period last year. Adjusted EBITDA improved 24% to negative $55 million from negative $72 million previously. Free cash flow deteriorated to negative $93 million but represented a 23% improvement. Capital expenditures declined to $40 million from $46 million. The company maintained $251 million in cash equivalents and marketable securities. Virgin Galactic successfully moved its first new spaceship from the assembly hangar to the test and launch facility. The company hired additional spaceship pilots and provided flight windows for approximately 650 founding astronauts. CEO Michael Colglazier noted strong early booking response to newly priced expeditions, with customer interest spanning over 20 countries. A rocket motor assembly line in Phoenix is under construction and expected to commence operations in Q4 2026. Second-quarter 2026 projections include approximately $100,000 in revenue and free cash flow between negative $87 million and $92 million.
